terms.lastUpdated: Last updated: July 10, 2026 · Effective upon publication
terms.introBefore: Welcome to Vinlyst. These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") govern access to and use of the Vinlyst web application (the "Service"), operated from Argentina. By creating an account or using the Service, you accept these Terms and our
terms.introAfter: in full. If you don't agree, you shouldn't use Vinlyst.

section1.heading: 1. Description of the service
section1.body: Vinlyst is a social network for music reviews. It lets users search albums and tracks from a catalog provided by external providers, publish reviews with a score from 1 to 10, rate individual tracks within an album, follow other users, comment on and react to reviews, and discover music through trending and personalized recommendation sections.
section1.calloutBold: Vinlyst doesn't host or distribute audio files.
section1.calloutRest: The 30-second previews and catalog metadata (cover art, titles, artists, duration) come from external providers and are shown solely to identify the content being reviewed.

section2.heading: 2. Eligibility and account creation
section2.body: To use Vinlyst you need to create an account with an email and password, or by authenticating with your Google account. You're responsible for keeping your credentials confidential and for all activity that occurs under your account.
section2.li1Suffix: ) must be between 3 and 30 characters, using only letters, numbers, and underscores, and may not infringe trademarks or impersonate third parties.
section2.li2: You must provide truthful information when registering and keep it up to date.
section2.li3: By registering, you give explicit, informed consent to the processing of your personal data under our Privacy Policy, logged with a date and time on your account.
section2.li4: Vinlyst reserves the right to suspend or delete accounts that breach these Terms.

section3.heading: 3. User-generated content
section3.intro: By posting a review, comment, profile bio, or any other content ("User Content"), you represent that:
section3.li1: It's yours, original, or you hold the rights necessary to publish it.
section3.li2: It doesn't contain defamatory, discriminatory, or harassing language, spam, unsolicited advertising, or false information presented as fact.
section3.li3: You grant Vinlyst a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to store, display, and distribute that content within the Service for as long as your account and the content remain active.
section3.body2: You retain ownership of your User Content. You can delete your reviews and comments individually at any time; deletion is logical and immediate in the interface.
section3.sub1BodyBefore: Track reviews carry a score from 1 to 10 assigned directly by the user. Album reviews calculate their final score as the
section3.sub1BodyBold: average of the scores assigned to each track
section3.sub1BodyAfter: included in the review; this value is calculated and stored at the moment the review is published or edited, and isn't recalculated retroactively if the user doesn't edit it again.

section4.heading: 4. Community guidelines and moderation
section4.body: Vinlyst is a space for exchanging opinions about music. We don't tolerate content that amounts to harassment, hate speech, spam or unauthorized advertising, deliberate disinformation, or impersonation.
section4.sub1Body1: Any user can report a review, comment, or account they consider to be in breach of these guidelines, stating a reason. Reports are reviewed by the Vinlyst moderation team, who may accept them (confirming the violation) or dismiss them.
section4.sub1Body2: When a report on a review or comment is accepted, that content is hidden from public view and its author is notified within the platform. When a report on an account is accepted, this hiding doesn't apply, but the penalty described below is still counted.
section4.sub2Body1: Vinlyst applies an automatic, escalating penalty system to accounts whose content or behavior accumulates reports accepted by moderation. Each accepted report on a user's content or account adds 1 to their cumulative count, even if several distinct reports refer to the same content.
section4.sub2Body2: Every 3 accepted reports, the account crosses a new level of temporary penalty, according to the following scale:

restrictions: 1 day unable to post reviews or comments

section4.sub2Body3: Each new penalty replaces the remaining time of a previous one; the periods don't stack. While a penalty is active, the account keeps read access, reactions, and the ability to follow other users, but can't create new reviews or comments. A suspended account — whether automatically by this system or manually by the Vinlyst team — can't sign in or continue using the Service.
section4.sub2Body4: Vinlyst reserves the right to suspend or delete any account manually, without needing to exhaust this scale, in the event of serious violations.

section5.heading: 5. Music catalog and external providers
section5.body1: The album, track, and artist information and the 30-second audio previews shown on Vinlyst are obtained from third-party music databases. Vinlyst doesn't own that metadata, doesn't guarantee its accuracy or continuous availability, and isn't affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by those providers or by the artists, record labels, or rights holders of the referenced works.
section5.body2: At the time a review is published, Vinlyst keeps a copy of the title, artist, and cover art of the reviewed album or track, so your review stays readable even if that information changes or becomes unavailable from the external provider.

section6.heading: 6. Notifications
section6.body: Vinlyst notifies you within the platform when another user "likes" or "dislikes" your review, comments on it, or starts following you, as well as for moderation decisions that affect your content or account. You can turn off each type of social notification individually, or all of them together, from your account settings. Notifications tied to moderation decisions about your account can't be turned off.

section7.heading: 7. Privacy and personal data protection
section7.body1: Vinlyst processes your personal data in accordance with Argentina's Law No. 25,326 on Personal Data Protection. We only collect the data needed to operate the Service: email, password (stored irreversibly via cryptographic hashing), username, handle, and optionally a bio and profile photo.
section7.rightsIntro: As the owner of your data, you have the right to:
section7.li1Rest: request and download a copy of all your stored data, reviews, and comments.
section7.li2Rest: correct your profile data at any time from your account settings.
section7.li3Rest: delete your account. Doing so anonymizes your identifiable personal data (email, handle, name, photo, bio, password) irreversibly. Your reviews and comments remain visible to preserve the context of community conversations, but are attributed to an anonymized account, with no data that could identify you.
section7.body2Before: Your passwords are stored using one-way cryptographic hashing functions; Vinlyst never has access to your password in plain text. All communications with the Service are encrypted via HTTPS. For full details on what data we collect, who we share it with, and how to exercise your rights, see our

section8.heading: 8. Intellectual property
section8.body: The name "Vinlyst," its logo, interface design, and the underlying software are the property of Vinlyst or its licensors. The titles, cover art, and recordings referenced in the catalog belong to their respective rights holders; their display on Vinlyst is for informational purposes, in the context of users' reviews and comments about those works.

section9.heading: 9. Limitation of liability
section9.body: The Service is provided "as is" and "as available." Vinlyst doesn't guarantee that the Service will be free of interruptions or errors, or that the third-party music catalog will always be available or up to date. Opinions expressed in reviews and comments belong solely to their authors and don't represent Vinlyst's position. To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, Vinlyst won't be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from use of the Service.

section10.heading: 10. Changes to these Terms
section10.body: We may update these Terms to reflect changes in the Service or in applicable regulations. For substantial changes, we'll notify registered users within the platform before they take effect. Continued use of the Service after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of them.

section11.heading: 11. Governing law and jurisdiction
section11.body: These Terms are governed by the laws of Argentina. Any dispute arising from their interpretation or performance will be submitted to the ordinary courts with jurisdiction over the Autonomous City of Buenos Aires, without prejudice to consumer protection rules that apply on a mandatory basis.

section12.heading: 12. Contact
section12.before: For questions about these Terms, to exercise your rights over your personal data, or to report a concern that can't be resolved through the in-platform reporting system, you can write to us at

privacy.lastUpdated: Last updated: July 10, 2026 · Effective upon publication
privacy.introBefore: This Privacy Policy describes what personal data Vinlyst collects, why we process it, who we share it with, and what rights you have over it, in accordance with Argentina's Law No. 25,326 on Personal Data Protection. It complements our
privacy.introAfter: , which you also accept by using the Service.

section1.heading: 1. Data controller
section1.before: Vinlyst is the controller responsible for the personal data it collects through the web application. For any questions about this policy or to exercise your rights, you can write to us at

section2.heading: 2. What data we collect

rows.origin: Direct registration (stored hashed, never in plain text)
rows.required: Yes, unless registering with Google only

rows.data: Device IP address and user agent
Records of reports created or received

section2.body: We don't request or store sensitive data (ethnic origin, health, political or religious opinions, sexual orientation) beyond what the user voluntarily chooses to include in their bio or reviews, at their sole responsibility.

section3.heading: 3. Purpose of processing
section3.li1: Create and authenticate your account, and keep your session active securely.
section3.li2: Let you publish and manage reviews, comments, and follow relationships with other users.
section3.li3: Show you a personalized feed with the activity of the accounts you follow.
section3.li4: Send you in-platform notifications about interactions with your content, according to your configured preferences.
section3.li5: Generate album and track recommendations based on your review history, once you reach a minimum of 3 published reviews.
section3.li6: Calculate trending sections from the community's aggregated, anonymous activity.
section3.li7: Prevent fraud, abuse, spam, and content that violates our community guidelines, including operating the reporting and penalty system.
section3.li8: Comply with legal obligations and respond to requests from competent authorities.

section4.heading: 4. Legal basis and consent
section4.body: The processing of your data is based on the informed, express consent you give when registering, through explicit acceptance of these documents. This consent is logged with a date and time on your account. You can withdraw your consent at any time by deleting your account, as described in section 8.

section5.heading: 5. Who we share data with
section5.intro: Vinlyst doesn't sell your personal data to third parties. We share limited information with the following providers, strictly to operate the Service:

rows.receives: Authentication request, if you choose to sign in with Google
rows.purpose: Verify your identity without sharing an additional password with us

rows.receives: Search terms for albums, tracks, and artists
rows.purpose: Show you music catalog results and audio previews; they don't receive your account data or the content of your reviews

rows.receives: Your profile photo, if you upload one
rows.purpose: Host and serve the image in an optimized way
Send you account verification, password recovery, and suspension notices

section5.callout: None of these providers receive your password. Music catalog searches are performed without linking your user identity to the query.

section6.heading: 6. Information security
section6.li1: Passwords are stored using one-way cryptographic hashing functions (never in plain text or in a reversible form).
section6.li2: All traffic between your device and our servers travels encrypted via HTTPS.
section6.li3: Access to the platform is managed with short-lived session tokens, securely renewed and automatically revoked upon suspicious activity.
section6.li4: We limit internal access to personal data to staff strictly necessary to operate and support the Service.
section6.body: In the event of a security breach that compromises personal data, we'll notify affected users and the relevant supervisory authority in accordance with applicable regulations, as soon as reasonably possible.

section7.heading: 7. Retention period
section7.body: We keep your personal data for as long as your account remains active. Reviews and comments are kept unless you delete them or delete your account, since they're part of the community's public history. Technical access logs (IP address, user agent) are kept for a limited period for security purposes and then purged.

section8.heading: 8. Your rights: access, rectification, and erasure
section8.intro: As the owner of your personal data, you can exercise the following rights at any time from your account settings, or by writing to us at
section8.li1Rest: you can download a complete copy of your stored personal data, reviews, and comments in an exportable format.
section8.li2Rest: you can correct your username, handle, bio, and profile photo at any time.
section8.li3Bold: Right of erasure (right to be forgotten):
section8.li3Rest: you can delete your account at any time. This action is irreversible.
section8.sub1Heading: 8.1 What happens when you delete your account
section8.sub1Body1: When you delete your account, we immediately run an irreversible anonymization process: your email, handle, and name are replaced with generic, non-identifiable values, your password is deleted, your profile photo and bio are removed, and all your active sessions are revoked.
section8.sub1Body2: Your already-published reviews, scores, and comments remain visible on the platform, so as not to break the context of other users' conversations and interactions, but they're attributed to that anonymized account — with no data that could identify you as their author again.

section9.heading: 9. Minors
section9.body: Vinlyst isn't directed at children under 13, and its use by minors under 18 requires supervision by a parent or legal guardian. We don't knowingly collect data from children under 13; if we become aware that this has happened, we'll delete that account and its associated data.

section10.heading: 10. Cookies and local storage
section10.body: Vinlyst uses browser local storage strictly necessary to keep you signed in and remember interface preferences (such as the navigation bar's state). We don't use advertising cookies or third-party tracking cookies for commercial purposes.

section11.heading: 11. International transfers
section11.body: Some of our infrastructure providers (hosting, database, image storage) may process data on servers located outside Argentina. In those cases, we contractually require those providers to maintain an adequate level of data protection, equivalent to that required by Law No. 25,326.

section12.heading: 12. Changes to this policy
section12.body: We may update this Privacy Policy to reflect changes in our practices or in applicable regulations. For substantial changes to how we handle your data, we'll notify you within the platform before they take effect.

section13.heading: 13. Supervisory authority
section13.body: You have the right to file a complaint with the Agency for Access to Public Information (AAIP), the supervisory body for Law No. 25,326, if you believe the processing of your personal data doesn't comply with applicable regulations.

section14.heading: 14. Contact
section14.body: For questions about this Privacy Policy or to exercise any of your rights, write to us at
